description: Allowing the batch or sequence length to grow unbounded, causing an out-of-memory crash at peak concurrency.
body: Failure mode: batching has no size or memory cap, so at peak concurrency with long inputs (or a large KV cache) the accelerator runs out of memory and the whole server crashes, dropping all in-flight requests. Detection signals: OOM errors correlated with traffic peaks; server restarts under load; memory graph hitting the ceiling. Do not batch without a memory-derived cap. Safer fallback: compute worst-case memory from max batch times max sequence plus weights, cap batch size so it fits, and queue or reject beyond capacity instead of OOM.
